chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C. P. No. D — 1713 of 2022. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE[S] 18.05.2022. FOR ORDERS ON OFFICE OBJECTION. FOR ORDERS ON M.A. 6003/2022. FOR HEARING OF MAIN CASE. Mr. Wazeer Hussain Khoso advocate for petitioner. ------- A similar petition No.D-1676 of 2022 involving the same controversy was decided of 17.05.2022, it involved the question that the respondent i.e. Executive Engineer Highway Division Matiari, should cancel the tender notice being non inclusive of the entire scheme as out of 51 works only 28 assignments were published for prospective award to the contractors. We have concluded in the above referred petition that there is no such vested right accrued to the petitioner to enforce the respondents to issue tender notices in respect of all such proposed schemes and unless an award has been made the petitioner cannot said to have a vested right. They may however, pursue their remedy for the public tenders of 28 assignments and may obtain tender document if not otherwise objected by the respondents. Petition, however, being devoid of merit as far as the question raised is concerned is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE A
